-- 1 of 4 --

The Seed Launch Part 3
!
Next up in the seed launch is the list. The beautiful thing about a seed launch is you
don't need a big list. You can do this with a list of 50 to 300 people. Bigger is okay, no
problem with that. Remember, the thing you're really doing with a seed launch is you
are creating a product. This is the fastest, easiest way to get a product.
!
If you're just starting out and you have a really small list, once you have the product it's
possible to go out and line up a JV partner or two and do a launch with them. Probably
not a full out JV launch, but with one or two partners. Then you can use that to build
your list. This is a way you can leapfrog.
!
You just need that little itty bitty list to get yourself a product and then you use that
product and the potential of affiliate income, or JV income, to pull in a couple of
affiliates. I've seen people do this over and over. Fifty to three hundred people is okay,
it's very easy to get that kind of list.
!
Go through the list-building training that's included in this course and you can start
contributing to some forums and become a valuable participant in some forums and
that can drive people back to your site, your squeeze page. You can give people an
ethical bribe and get people on your list.
!
Put up a few videos on YouTube, that can easily drive a few hundred people. Use
Facebook and Twitter, find people that are interested in your topic. Start providing value
to them and bingo, you can build this sizable list very easily. I just absolutely don't want
to hear anyone telling me you don't have a list, because to go get a list in the 50 to 300
people range is easy.
!
You just create a bribe, you create a squeeze page, you go use those traffic sources I
just told you about and you drive people there. You start building a list. Once you have
that sizable list, remember you want 30-60 people in your program, going through your
seed launch, to give you the interactivity so you can learn how to teach.
!
They can help you create the product. It becomes an interactive thing. It's a lot more
interesting to go through this process and teach a group of people than it is to try and
make it up on your own. Smaller is okay, you could do a smaller group of people if
they're really motivated.
!
The people are almost always less motivated than you think and that's why you need
30-60 people, to get them on your calls. Thirty is on the low end, 60 is better. You want
to get hopefully 60 people to sign up so that way you have 30 people really taking part
and interacting with you.
!
If you have some type of a group, for whatever reason you have this group, and you
just know they're incredibly rabid. They really are going to show up and your course
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2
PLF_Module1v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 2 of 4 --

isn't going to be too long. You might be able to get away with 10 or 12 people. In
general, you want that 30-60.
!
One other thing about this is if you look at the way I did it. With my Product Launch
Workshop, when I first did this, I sold somewhere in the range of six to nine people. I
wasn't terribly successful, but I ended up filling it out through my contacts. Through
people that were on my mastermind groups, or people that I just knew, friends of mine,
who were really interested in that. I was able to get it more in that 30-40 person range.
That ended up working out very well for me.
!
You should look at your contacts. Maybe you know people who are interested in your
topic, people you correspond with, just people you have a relationship with. If you
could fill out 10-20 of those people. You're going to get better compliance with them.
!
They're going to be more involved, in general, in your program. If you could find 10 or
20 of those people, then you only need to find 10 or 20 other, so called, strangers –
people from your list. That's another way to look at this.
!
Now let's look at the timeline. This is just a very rough, general timeline. You don't have
to follow this. This has just worked for me in the past, a couple times. You can move
these days around. You don't need a super long pre-launch.
!
Day One's initial announcement, that's the start of your pre-launch.
!
Day Three, you'll usually follow up. Hopefully you got some questions from that initial
announcement. Maybe that initial announcement was actually a survey shot across the
bow. You follow up a couple days later, you answer some questions.
!
Day Five, you'll start to really reveal what's going on with the program, giving some
more details about the program.
!
Day Seven, you release it, you send them to a sales letter or a sales video. Generally it
doesn't have to be super extensive, because you're doing this to a smaller, warmer list.
It's more like an interaction with them. You release the program on Day Seven.
!
Day Eight, if you don't fill it up and sell out immediately then you follow up. Maybe you
follow up with some social proof.
!
Day Nine, follow up with some scarcity and social proof. The scarcity is they have to
sign up by the next day or the program's going offline, you're going to shut down the
ordering process.
!
You're basically looking at a four-day period that the curtain's open – Day Seven, Eight,
Nine, and Ten.
!

PLF_Module3v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 3 of 4 --

Day Nine, you give them scarcity, “Hey, we're going to shut down the registration
tomorrow. A lot of people have already jumped on board, they're very excited about
this and I'm really excited to start teaching this program and I'm going to have to pull it
off the market. I'm going to shut down registration tomorrow, because the class starts
the next day.”
!
Then on Day Ten, you follow up with another scarcity message, some additional social
proof, and you close down the program. You might even put out two emails on that day,
on Day Ten, early in the day and later in the day. “Hey, we're closing it down in just a
few hours.”
!
Then the next day you start the program. You don't have to start it the next day, you
can start it a couple days later. Then typically I'll do another call or another module
every week. I've got Day 11, Day 18, Day 25.
!
How many modules you do is completely up to you. I would suggest doing at least
three. Three, four, five, is a nice sweet spot. You could do three with a question and
answer session, so that gives you four, or four with a Q&A or five with a Q&A. You could
go crazy and have 12 modules, but you probably don't want to stretch out a seed
launch and a seed program quite that long. I would say the magic is somewhere
between three and five modules.
!
Some people do this and they release it every two or three days. I've seen people do
this where they do it a Monday, Wednesday, Friday, and get it all over within one week.
It really depends on your market, what you want to do, how meaty it is, how lengthy,
how much you have to teach.
!
Then basically, immediately after the program ends, then you start building out your
product for your full launch. You can take the recordings about what you did, edit them,
make them look better, get them transcribed. Add in any bonus material and then you
have yourself a product.
!
Or you can do what I did with the original Product Launch Workshop, I just took
everything that I taught and I redid it 100%. The original one was just a bunch of
teleseminars. I redid it as screen capture and I actually turned it into a physical product
with CD's and DVD's and I built it out into a big product. You can be the perfectionist
like me, but you're probably better off just doing the quick and dirty and getting the
product out there.
